title: No max-input-size guard in lexer or form parser
description: SCENARIO: tokenize() and read_script() accept arbitrarily large input. A 1 GB Sieve script body (e.g. a huge multiline text: block) will allocate proportional memory with no rejection. In a server context (Dovecot plugin, web filter API) an attacker can send oversized scripts to exhaust memory. MAINTENANCE CONSEQUENCE: no denial-of-service protection at the library boundary; callers must add their own guards. Fix: document that the library does not enforce input size limits and callers are responsible, OR add a configurable max-size parameter to compile().
